title: Talking about my life - Boulder, Colorado 13/21

transcript.text: Yeah, I did the next race after that, but I, like, went to the bathroom too late, and when I came out, they had already staged it, and they had the juniors racing with, starting with the beginners, so it was this huge pack, so I had to start the race at the very back of the pack, and it was a really short race, and you go up this dirt road a little bit, and then it turns into, like, paved roads, so I had to, like, fight my way through all these beginners, and, you know, I got myself through most of them, but then I got stuck by a couple of them on the single track on the climb,

And then I passed them on the downhill, and then I finally caught up to the dude who's in first place, Ben Marshall, and I caught up to him, like, right, like, a little bit, like, on the climb on the dirt road, and he looks back at me, and I'm like, what's up? He's like, so then I was riding with him, but by then I had kind of burnt myself out, catching up to him, so he, like, dropped me before the single track, and then rode away from me on the single track, he was gone, I was like, fuck, man, I missed my chance,

But then I went down, and, like, I was riding down, and, like, I made a wrong turn, some dude was like, no, no, not here, and I was like, what, and I, like, he jumped out of the way, and I zoomed through, and I made, like, a shortcut on accident, and so then, like, I had caught up to Ben Marshall again, and he was in front of me,

And then I zoomed by him, like, he was standing still on, like, the last corner, right before the finish, and I won that race, but that was, like, the last race I ever did, I think it was the last race I ever did, it was either that one, yeah, that was the last race I ever did, last mountain bike race,
And, um, because I didn't really have a good year that year, and then my dad encouraged me not to race anymore, because it was too expensive, my dad insisted on driving me to all the races,

Which made it more expensive, and so he was just like, don't race next year, dude, and I was like, alright, whatever, okay, so the next year I didn't race, but, um, but yeah, I wasn't very good, I didn't, I felt like I had really bad luck, like, I was
Always getting mechanicals, every time I showed up at a race, like, a pulley would fall out, or I would
Crack something, I cracked a frame in a race, I, um, that was in, um, Crested Butte race, Crested Butte was a really big race, we went up there,
And, um, my dad drove me out, I was 15, so this was, like, 90, and, um, we started in this huge pack, and Luke Miller was there, he was this kid I'd been racing, like, the Red Singer with
For a while, and we were, like, hanging out, and we rode, and there was this huge pack, and then it funneled into this
Single track, and my chain fell off, so I had to, like, put it back on, and then, and then we were riding, and then we got to this
Climb, and there was this girl named, like, Elodie Brown, who was only, like, a year older than me, and she was, like, a top expert racer, I think she
Turned into pro after that, but she was, like, right in front of me, but she, like, rode away from me, she dropped me on the
Climb, so I got beat by a girl, um, and then, uh, and then the next race, but I was, I was in the lead for
Junior, because that kid, Ben Allen, was actually ahead of me, but then I saw him on the climb, like, barely
Moving, he, like, bonked or something, and, and then the next race was this, like, just, like, this site
Criterium around Crested Butte on dirt roads and stuff, and, um, and, like, that kid, Ben Allen, was, like, blew me
Away, he, like, dropped me, I ended up winning that overall anyway, because I'd beat him by a lot the
Day before, but I was, like, what the hell is wrong with my bike, I'm barely moving, it's totally
Squishy, what the fuck, like, are my, and I looked at my tires, and it didn't look like there was no air
In my tires, and I finished the race, and I was, like, what the hell, and then I look at my bike, and
The frame was cracked, totally cracked on the diagonal tube thing coming up, so I was lucky that the top
Frame didn't crack, or I would have had a spill and, like, done a faceplant, gotten one of these,
But, um, yeah, I won a helmet on that one, but anyways, yeah, I did a, what other races did I do?
We did Winter Park, Frisco, there was this Frisco race, which was crap, those idiots, the directors
Didn't know what the fuck they were doing, like, I won that race, I should have won that race,
But I didn't, I thought the finish line was the start line, because they had no direction
Of where it was, and when I finally went to the finish line, the other kid had already
Finished, and so I ended up getting third to my brother, that pissed me off, and then
The year before that, I won it, but I got disqualified, because I crossed the finish line from the wrong
Direction, so they fucking disqualified me for that, so that the son of the race director
Ended up winning, so after that, I was, like, fuck these guys, I actually yelled at the guy,
And he's, like, I was, like, you ass, you morons, and he, like, yelled back at me, and I was, like,
Fuck you, and, and I think they canceled it after that, because there was this big backlash against
Them, like, all the racers, like, um, like, rebelled against it, um, but, um, yeah, but what else,
We did the Breckenridge every year, because that's where our trailer was, we did Durango,
Durango, I did really bad at Durango, though, I crashed on that one once, oh, we did, uh, this,
Um, Colorado Springs, I got second on that one, I beat Ben Allen, because I dropped him on the
Downhill before the final, like, dirt row that went up, this kid named Paul, something or other,
One, I was hanging out with him after that, and, uh, so I was an okay mountain bike racer,
I was a crappy road racer, though, um, so, yeah, that was, that's my bike racing days as a kid,
I never rode with anybody, though, um, I rode with my brother, though, sometimes, my brother
Didn't like riding with me, because he just didn't like me generally back then, he thought I was,
Like, a nerd, because I didn't have any friends, so he kind of wanted to, like, add to that,
He just got really pissed off at me, but he did let me ride with him, we went mountain bike rides
With him and stuff, and his buddies, he had a lot of, like, bike racing buddies, like,
Who were really good, like, Arian Wheeler, Dave Alonji, and JP,
Diaboim, they rode all the time, and they were badass, like, Arian won the Red Zinger twice,
Dave won stages, JP almost won a stage, and was, like, getting fifth and stuff from the hill climbs,
My brother won a bunch of, won a few stages, he won the time trial of LNCAR and Boulder Mall Criterium twice
In the first stage of the 89 Red Zinger and the Criterium in Boulder, and, um, and I didn't,
But I didn't do group rides either, I should have done group rides, it was fucked up, I remember,
I only remember one group ride I did with my friends, with my, or with my brother and his friends,
It was this woman's group ride, it was a girl's group ride, but it was a big one, there was, like,
Shitloads of girls there, I was actually talking to some girl at the beginning, and she's like,
Oh, you're a mountain biker, we call it maintenance biking, because it's just a pain in the ass,
I was like, yeah, so then we, like, dropped all those girls, we, yeah, I think we dropped all the
Girls, because next thing I know, it was, like, just us, and off the front of them, and,
And then Wyndham kind of dropped me, and I was second, and then there was Arian, and,
Yeah, Wyndham was actually, he was a better rider than me there for a couple years,
He could drop me, a better road rider, probably not a mountain biker, but he rode a lot more than
I did, though, so, um, yeah, so that's the bike racing, um, yeah, so then I joined, so then after
Sixth grade, Uni Hill, that's when we went into, uh, what's it called, uh, junior high school,
And I guess after we ended junior high school, they turned it into, uh, middle school, which is
Sixth grade to ninth grade, but when we were there, and then high school would be ninth grade to twelfth
Grade, but, but the way I did it is, we went from seventh grade to ninth grade, and then tenth grade
To twelfth grade, so seventh grade to ninth grade was baseline junior high, and that was, like, two
Blocks from where I lived, where my house was, so, which I thought was cool, because I just got to,
Like, ride my bike, or, like, walk, I, yeah, I, like, ride, like, a BMX bike to school, it was, like,
Really close, and, uh, so my friends there in seventh grade, at the very beginning were due,
Because he was my friend, the Korean kid, he was my friend from the year before in sixth grade,
But then he started hanging out with this kid named Mike Winger, and, uh, who's this blonde-haired
Kid, and he was, like, his best friend, and I was, like, I was, like, I don't know, man, if I want to
Hang out with you guys, because I'm not going to, like, be your buddy if I'm not going to be, like,
If I'm going to be, like, the second buddy to, like, that other kid, or whatever, so I was, like, I stopped
Hanging out with him, but I started hanging out with him again in, like, ninth grade, but, um,
Seventh grade, I didn't hang out with anybody, because at lunchtime, which is the time where you
Hang out with your friends, I just went home, I had this off-campus pass, and I just went home and
Had lunch there, but then my parents, like, got pissed off at me for doing that, so they, like,
Started telling me that I wasn't allowed to do that anymore, so I had to, like, start having lunch at
School, but I didn't want to hang out with anybody, I was, like, really, like, I don't know what it was,
But I, I started getting really paranoid, and, uh, I just didn't want to, I did, I didn't want to try
And, I wasn't in any group, like, they had already formed all the different cliques, and I hadn't
Joined myself into a clique by then, so it was kind of, I felt like it was too late, so I didn't have any
Friends, so I just, like, hung out at lunch all by myself in the hallway, and I remember some girls,
I think there were, like, girls from, like, eighth grade or something were, like, are you new? I was,
Like, no, you're, like, oh, well, because you're alone, I was, like, yeah, damn parents won't let me
Have lunch at their house, but, yeah, I didn't really like junior high, junior high wasn't fun for
Me, because, um, you just, I don't know, I didn't, I didn't like the setup, you'd have all these
Different classes, it's, like, seven different classes throughout the day, and it's different
Subjects, and so you go in there, and then the teacher, like, is telling you about something,
But they tell you about it as if, as if you're retarded, like, they were talking, I felt like
They were talking to us as if we were retarded, I mean, we had this guy named Mr. Tidwell teaching
Geography, and it would take us, like, five days to get through, like, two pages, like, we'd spend,
Like, two days on stalagmite tights and stalagmites, you know, and he's, like, does anybody know what
Stalagtite or stalagmite is, and it's, like, yes, you know, it was, like, fucking boring, man, like,
Um, he was, like, this hillbilly dude, and, yeah, they just went by so fast that I was just, like,
I think I, that's, that's about when I started having trouble staying awake, like, they were,
Like, hypnotizing me, like, I would be, like, yawning, and, like, almost, I remember, like,
My head would go into free fall, and this happened all the way through college, and I think I started,
It started back then, but it'd go all the way through college, where your head goes into free
Fall, and then you catch it when you're, like, this far, and I always had this, like, fear that I would,
Like, bang my head against the desk, or, like, bang my head against the floor, um, and, like,
Injure myself, and, um, and, oh, yeah, and then, and then the eyes, you can't keep your eyes open,
Like, you feel totally fucking awake, you feel great, you go into school, and this happened in
College, too, this happened all the way through the end of college, you feel totally great, and then
You go into class, 10 minutes into class, you're doing this, like, all, you have to focus all of
Your energy just to keep your eyes open, like, there's this energy that comes in that just knocks
You out, it's like taking 10 sleeping pills, and in college, I actually resorted to drinking, like,
Five coffees before every class, just so I wouldn't have to deal with that, because you can't pay
Attention to what the teacher's saying if you're focusing on trying to keep your eyes open, and your
Head from falling, I mean, you're passing out, but, uh, yeah, but I didn't have, so by the time
School got out, I was, like, dazed, and exhausted, and dehydrated, maybe, because I guess I forgot
To drink water, because I remember feeling really ill physically, like, dehydrated, and
Hungry, and tired, so I'd just go home, and, like, have a big bowl of cereal, and, like, just
Be, like, totally bonked, like, low blood sugar, and I think I, I had to get, like, diagnosed
With, like, Epstein-Barr at one point, well, this was back in 11th grade, this was in 1990
Where I got diagnosed with that, but I was just, like, tired, man, and so I, yeah, that's
Why I, I didn't really ride as much, bike ride as much as I, as I wanted to, you know,
Every year I was, like, I'm gonna ride so much this year, and I never did, and school would
Get out, and I'd just be, like, and, um, yeah, so then we had this other teacher named
Mrs. something or other, what was her name, Mrs. Robertson, or Robinson, she's this, like,
Really old lady, she's, like, 65 or something, and she was, like, senile, I think kids were
Bragging about how they would, like, they would, like, drop, like, extra sugar tablets or, like,
Something in her, on her coffee, and she wouldn't even notice, and she was our, like, language
Arts teacher, and, um, she, like, she was, yeah, she'd just teach us, like, punctuation
And stuff, and you're just supposed to, I hated school, because you're just supposed
To, like, sit there and watch the teacher, like, draw on the board, you know, and then
You're, like, supposed to sit there and then, like, do the thing on your paper, and it's,
Like, it didn't, it made me want to rebel against school, so I didn't want to do any of the
Homework, and, um, and I remember looking at some of the other kids, like, especially
Girls, and their papers would be fucking, like, folded perfectly, and I, I remember
Being, like, okay.
